mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and potential complexity in configuration for distributed setups.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the vLLM command-line interface to manage deployments.

```bash

pip install vllm==0.18.0

```

> Install the specific version of vLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Create a GKE cluster with the required specifications for running vLLM.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create vllm-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust the region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with cluster credentials.
commands: gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region us-central1

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in 'Ready' status.

steps.title: Deploy vLLM
steps.description:
Deploy the vLLM application using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.0

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.0

```

> Add the vLLM Helm repository.
> Install vLLM in the specified namespace.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for your vLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment vllm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

kubectl apply -f monitoring-config.yaml

```

> Enable autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> Apply your monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
Remove the vLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

gcloud container clusters delete vllm-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command removes the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the GKE cluster and all associated resources.
commands: gcloud container clusters delete vllm-cluster --region us-central1 --quiet

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Backup your current configuration, upgrade vLLM, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get deployment vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.0

kubectl rollout status deployment/vllm --namespace vllm

```

> Backup the current deployment configuration.
> Upgrade vLLM to the specified version.
> Verify that the upgrade was successful.
commands: kubectl get deployment vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough CPU resources to schedule the vLLM pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the number of nodes or the machine type in your GKE cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM application is failing to start due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the vLLM pods using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for detailed error messages.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The service is not properly exposed or the pod is not running.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the vLLM service is correctly configured and the pods are in 'Running' state.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new quantization methods and improved memory management.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods --namespace vllm' showing all pods in 'Running' status.
codeSnippets: gcloud container clusters get-credentials vllm-cluster --region us-central1

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
